prevalence.value: ~2-4% (adults; higher with age and risk factors)
prevalence.source: Cardiovascular epidemiology syntheses; ischemic heart disease context (India)
prevalence.trend: stable; high CAD burden and aging increase absolute cases

incidence.population: Adults at risk for coronary artery disease
incidence.source: Stable ischemic heart disease cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to disability and progression risk to MI/heart failure
burden.mortalityRate: Low per se; driven by underlying CAD events
burden.source: IHME/GBD ischemic heart disease burden context

vulnerablePopulations: lower-income groups with limited access to secondary prevention
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical exertional chest discomfort relieved by rest/nitrates; ECG and stress testing; coronary imaging/angiography for high-risk features; differentiate from ACS

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening for angina; variable cardiovascular risk screening
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improved by access to guideline-based therapy and revascularization when needed

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Testing access is uneven; many diagnoses are clinical in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antianginals (beta-blockers, nitrates, CCBs), antiplatelet/statin, BP/diabetes control, lifestyle change, cardiac rehab when available; PCI/CABG for selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed presentation, uneven access to stress testing/angiography outside major cities, cost barriers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide availability of affordable generics; large cardiology networks in major metros
